name: wetlab
documentation:
# `wetlab`: Default wetlab schema

Read the docs: [docs.lamin.ai/wetlab](https://docs.lamin.ai/wetlab).

classes.name: wetlab.migrations.0018_squashed.Migration
documentation: Functions from the following migrations need manual copying.
Move them and any dependencies into this file, then update the
RunPython operations to refer to the local versions:

modules.name: wetlab
documentation:
Basic wetlab entities \[[source](https://github.com/laminlabs/wetlab/blob/main/wetlab/models.py)\].

Install and mount `wetlab` in a new instance:

```pycon
>>> pip install wetlab
>>> lamin init --storage ./test-wetlab --schema bionty,wetlab
```

Import the package:

```pycon
>>> import wetlab as wl
```

Create records:

```pycon
>>> compound_treatment = wl.CompoundTreatment(
...    name="Aspirin treatment day 1",
... ).save()
```

Registries:

```
.. autosummary::
   :toctree: .

   Experiment
   Biosample
   Techsample
   CombinationTreatment
   CompoundTreatment
   EnvironmentalTreatment
   GeneticTreatment
   TreatmentTarget
   Well
```

classes.name: wetlab.models.Compound

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Compound.id
documentation: Internal id, valid only in one DB instance.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Compound.uid
documentation: A universal id (hash of selected field).

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Compound.chembl_id
documentation: Chembl ontology ID of the compound

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Compound.synonyms
documentation: Bar-separated (|) synonyms that correspond to this compound.

Models a (chemical) compound such as a drug.

Examples:
    >>> compound = wl.Compound(
    ...    name="Navitoclax",
    ...    ontology_id="CHEMBL443684"
    ... ).save()
if instance.pk is not None and related_manager.exists():

classes.name: wetlab.models.Experiment

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Experiment.id
documentation: Internal id, valid only in one DB instance.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Experiment.uid
documentation: Universal id, valid across DB instances.

Models a wetlab experiment.

Example:
    >>> experiment = wl.Experiment(
    ...     name="IPF mice vs control mice",
    ...     description="Analysis of gene expression levels in different cell types of IPF.",
    ... ).save()

classes.name: wetlab.models.Well

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Well.id
documentation: Internal id, valid only in one DB instance.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Well.uid
documentation: Universal id, valid across DB instances.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Well.row
documentation: Horizontal position of the well in the microplate.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Well.column
documentation: Vertical position of the well in the microplate.

Models a well in a wetlab {class}`wetlab.Experiment` that is part of a microplate.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> well = wl.Well(
  ...    name="Well A1",
  ...    row="A",
  ...    column=1,
  ... ).save()
  ```

classes.name: wetlab.models.TreatmentTarget

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.TreatmentTarget.id
documentation: Internal id, valid only in one DB instance.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.TreatmentTarget.uid
documentation: Universal id, valid across DB instances.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.TreatmentTarget.genes
documentation: Genes of the treatment target, link to {class}`~bionty.Gene` records.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.TreatmentTarget.pathways
documentation: Pathways of the treatment target, link to {class}`bionty.Pathway` records.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.TreatmentTarget.artifacts
documentation: Artifacts linked to the treatment target.

Models treatment targets such as {class}`~bionty.Gene`, {class}`~bionty.Pathway`, and {class}`~bionty.Protein`.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> gene_1 = bt.Gene.from_source(ensembl_gene_id="ENSG00000000003").save()
  >>> gene_2 = bt.Gene.from_source(ensembl_gene_id="ENSG00000000005").save()
  >>> targets = wl.TreatmentTarget(name="TSPAN6_TNMD").save()
  >>> targets.genes.set([gene_1, gene_2])
  ```

classes.name: wetlab.models.GeneticTreatment

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.GeneticTreatment.id
documentation: Internal id, valid only in one DB instance.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.GeneticTreatment.uid
documentation: Universal id, valid across DB instances.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.GeneticTreatment.system
documentation: System used for the genetic treatment.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.GeneticTreatment.on_target_score
documentation: On-target score, indicating the likelihood of the guide RNA successfully targeting the intended DNA sequence.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.GeneticTreatment.off_target_score
documentation: The off-target score, indicating the likelihood of the guide RNA targeting unintended DNA sequences..

Models Genetic perturbations such as CRISPR.

Args:
    name: The name of the genetic treatment.
    system: The system used to apply the genetic treatment.
            Must be one of 'CRISPR Cas9', 'CRISPRi', 'CRISPRa', 'shRNA', 'siRNA', 'transgene', 'transient transfection'.
    on_target_score: The on-target score, indicating the likelihood of the guide RNA successfully targeting the intended DNA sequence.
    off_target_score: The off-target score, indicating the likelihood of the guide RNA targeting unintended DNA sequences.

Examples:
    >>> sicke_cell_treatment = wl.GeneticTreatment(
    ...     system="CRISPR Cas9",
    ...     name="Hemoglobin Sickle Cell Treatment",
    ...     sequence="AGCTGACCGTGA",
    ...     on_target_score=85,
    ...     off_target_score=15
    ... ).save()

classes.name: wetlab.models.CompoundTreatment

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.CompoundTreatment.id
documentation: Internal id, valid only in one DB instance.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.CompoundTreatment.uid
documentation: Universal id, valid across DB instances.

Models compound treatments such as drugs.

Args:
    name: The name of the compound treatment.

Examples:
    >>> aspirin_treatment = compound_treatment = wl.CompoundTreatment(
    ...    name="Antibiotic cocktail",
    ... ).save()

classes.name: wetlab.models.EnvironmentalTreatment

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.EnvironmentalTreatment.id
documentation: Internal id, valid only in one DB instance.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.EnvironmentalTreatment.uid
documentation: Universal id, valid across DB instances.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.EnvironmentalTreatment.ontology_id
documentation: Ontology ID (EFO) of the environmental treatment.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.EnvironmentalTreatment.value
documentation: The value of the environmental treatment such as a temperature.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.EnvironmentalTreatment.unit
documentation: Unit of the value such as 'degrees celsius'

Models environmental perturbations such as heat, acid, or smoke treatments.

Args:
    name: Name of the environmental treatment.
    ontology_id: Ontology ID of the environmental treatment (EFO).
    value: A value such as a temperature.
    unit: A unit such as 'degrees celsius'.
    duration: Time duration of how long the treatment was applied.

Examples:
    >>> acid_treatment = EnvironmentalTreatment(
    ...     name='Acid Treatment',
    ...     ontology_id='EFO:0004416',
    ...     value=1.5,
    ...     unit='pH',
    ... ).save()

classes.name: wetlab.models.CombinationTreatment

methods.name: wetlab.models.CombinationTreatment.members
documentation: Retrieve all related GeneticTreatment, CompoundTreatment, and EnvironmentalTreatment instances.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.CombinationTreatment.id
documentation: Internal id, valid only in one DB instance.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.CombinationTreatment.uid
documentation: Universal id, valid across DB instances.

Combination of several Treatments.

CombinationTreatments model several Treatments jointly such as one or more {class}`wetlab.GeneticTreatment`,
{class}`wetlab.CompoundTreatment`, and {class}`wetlab.EnvironmentalTreatment` records.

Args:

: name: A name of the CombinationTreatment that summarizes all applied Treatments.
  description: A description of the CombinationTreatment.
  ontology_id: An ontology ID of the CombinationTreatment.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> sc_treatment = wl.GeneticTreatment(
  ...     system="CRISPR Cas9",
  ...     name="Hemoglobin Sickle Cell Treatment",
  ...     sequence="AGCTGACCGTGA",
  ... ).save()
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> cftr_treatment = wl.GeneticTreatment(
  ...     system="CRISPR Cas9",
  ...     name="Cystic Fibrosis CFTR Correction",
  ...     sequence="TTGGTGGTGAACT",
  ... ).save()
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> aspirin_treatment = compound_treatment = wl.CompoundTreatment(
  ...    name="Aspirin",
  ...    pubchem_id=2244
  ... ).save()
  ```

  ```pycon
  >>> comb_treatment = wl.CombinationTreatment(name="Hemoglobin Sickle Cell and CFTR Correction with Aspirin",
  ...    description="Targets both sickle cell anemia and cystic fibrosis, using CRISPR Cas9 and Aspirin for anti-inflammatory support."
  ... ).save()
  >>> comb_treatment.genetics.set([sc_treatment, aspirin_treatment])
  >>> comb_treatment.compounds.add(aspirin_treatment)
  ```

classes.name: wetlab.models.Biosample

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Biosample.id
documentation: Internal id, valid only in one DB instance.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Biosample.uid
documentation: Universal id, valid across DB instances.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Biosample.cell_lines
documentation: Cell lines linked to the biosample.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Biosample.cell_types
documentation: Cell types linked to the biosample.

Models a specimen derived from an organism, such as tissue, blood, or cells.

Examples:
    >>> biosample = wl.Biosample(
    ...     name="control",
    ...     batch="ctrl_1"
    ... ).save()

classes.name: wetlab.models.Techsample

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Techsample.id
documentation: Internal id, valid only in one DB instance.

class_variables.name: wetlab.models.Techsample.uid
documentation: Universal id, valid across DB instances.

Models technical samples which represent a processed or derived sample in a lab created from raw biological materials.

Examples:
    >>> techsample = wl.Techsample(
    ...     name="tech_1",
    ...     batch="replicates_3"
    ... ).save()
